exampleSentences: Anh ấy phải nghỉ học do bị bệnh nặng.
Do công việc quá bận nên tôi không thể đi chơi.
Chuyến bay bị hoãn do thời tiết xấu ở Hà Nội.
notes:
do = due to, because of. It is a conjunction used to explain the reason or cause of an event.

While it can be used like lý do (reason), its most common role in daily Vietnamese is as a connector. It’s slightly more formal than vì.

Structure: do + noun/phrase + nên + result.
notes_md:
**do** = *due to, because of*. It is a conjunction used to explain the reason or cause of an event.

While it can be used like *lý do* (reason), its most common role in daily Vietnamese is as a connector. It’s slightly more formal than *vì*.


Structure: **do + noun/phrase + nên + result.**
exampleSentencesTranslated: He had to miss school due to a serious illness.
Because of a busy workload, I cannot go out.
The flight was delayed due to bad weather in Hanoi.
sentenceBreakdowns: Anh ấy (he) + phải (must) + nghỉ học (miss school) + do (due to) + bị bệnh (sick/ill) + nặng (serious)
Do (Because of) + công việc (work) + quá bận (too busy) + nên (so) + tôi (I) + không thể (cannot) + đi chơi (go out)
Chuyến bay (the flight) + bị hoãn (delayed) + do (due to) + thời tiết (weather) + xấu (bad) + ở Hà Nội (in Hanoi)

content.sentence: Học sinh nghỉ học ___ trời mưa to.

translation.en: Students missed school due to heavy rain.
